message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Adding error_threshold parameter to quantize() method - need to edit Image.py quantize() and its tests. What files does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: I am implementing sort_palette parameter for Image.quantize (feature: Add custom sorting for palette colors). I need to edit src/PIL/Image.py and Tests/test_image_quantize.py. What files does your feature touch?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Our features (error_threshold vs sort_palette) both touch quantize() in Image.py and its tests, so full file-level disjointness isn't possible. Since we're on separate repo copies, I'll proceed with my error_threshold changes, keeping edits minimal/localized to the error_threshold param, new helper logic, and its docstring/tests, so our diffs merge cleanly later. Will not touch sort_palette-related code.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: We both need to touch quantize() in the same files, so a disjoint file split isn't possible here - only a disjoint ownership of regions. Proposal: I'll go first. I'll add the sort_palette param (new kwarg at end of quantize() signature, plus a small post-processing block after palette is built, plus new tests appended to test_image_quantize.py). I will NOT touch any error_threshold-related code. Once I send DONE, the files are yours to add error_threshold on top of my changes - please add your kwarg after mine in the signature and append your tests after mine, so our diffs don't overlap on the same lines. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Sounds good. I'll wait for your DONE message before editing quantize() signature/body, then add error_threshold kwarg after your sort_palette kwarg, and append my tests after yours.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Agreed. I'll add sort_palette as a new kwarg to quantize(), keep it isolated to its own code block/tests, and avoid touching error_threshold logic. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Finished sort_palette implementation: added sort_palette kwarg to quantize() (as the last positional-or-keyword param, right after dither), plus a new private helper _reorder_palette(self, sort_palette) right after quantize() in Image.py, and appended new tests (test_quantize_sort_palette_*) at the end of test_image_quantize.py. All existing + new tests pass. Go ahead and add error_threshold after my kwarg in the signature and after my tests in the test file.
